perturbation.original_text: Vector hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the parties, that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and that Vector and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) Vector may make any disclosure of such information to which Captaris gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed by Vector to its Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating and implementing a possible transaction between the parties, who shall keep such information confidential and who are provided with a copy of this letter agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Vector hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the parties, that the Evaluation Material should be kept confidential to a reasonable degree but in case of external audit, data can be disclosed , and that Vector and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) Vector may make any disclosure of such information to which Captaris gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed by Vector to its Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating and implementing a possible transaction between the parties, who shall keep such information confidential and who are provided with a copy of this letter agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates strict confidentiality. The change introduces an exception: 'the Evaluation Material should be kept confidential to a reasonable degree but in case of external audit, data can be disclosed'. This contradicts the initial statement about non-disclosure 'in any manner whatsoever', as it now allows for disclosure under certain circumstances (external audit) without a clear definition of 'reasonable degree'. The contradiction surfaces when the contract specifies remedies for breach which assume no level of disclosure is permitted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this letter agreement by either party or any of its Representatives and that the parties shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this letter agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant contradiction. The original text states that the evaluation material will not be disclosed 'in any manner whatsoever.' The changed text allows for disclosure in the case of an external audit ('data can be disclosed'). This directly contradicts the original non-disclosure agreement. The contradicted text specifies that 'money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach,' and that the parties are entitled to seek equitable relief. This remedy is incompatible with the revised text's allowance for disclosure under certain circumstances, as it presumes no level of disclosure. Thus, the change is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction, it will promptly inform the other party of that decision.
perturbation.changed_text: If either party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a transaction, it may or may not promptly inform the other party of that decision, subject to internal strategic considerations.
perturbation.explanation: The original text creates an obligation to 'promptly inform' the other party. The modified text makes this notification discretionary ('may or may not'). This contradicts the subsequent paragraph, which implies reliance on timely communication to trigger the return or destruction of Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In that case, or any time upon the request of a party for any reason, Vector will promptly, at Vector’s option (i) return to Captaris and/or (ii) destroy all Evaluation Material (and all tangible copies thereof) furnished to it or its Representatives by or on behalf of the other party pursuant hereto, and in either case no copies thereof shall be retained except that, any of the foregoing notwithstanding, that portion of the Evaluation Material that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents prepared by Vector’s Representatives, such Representatives’ agents, attorneys or employees, and oral or electronic Evaluation Material will be held by such Representatives and kept subject to the terms of this agreement or destroyed to the extent practicable and permitted by law.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that a party 'will promptly inform' the other if they do not wish to proceed. The changed text modifies this to 'may or may not promptly inform,' creating a discretionary action. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which requires timely notification from a party to trigger the process of returning or destroying the Evaluation Material. The contradiction stems from the changed text making the notification optional when the contradicted text relies on its certainty for the operation of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: Captaris agrees that, for a period ending eight weeks after the date of this letter agreement, it will afford Vector and its Representatives, reasonable access to the information described in Exhibit A and the books, contracts, commitments and records of Captaris and its subsidiaries relevant thereto, unless and until Vector and Captaris have reached a non-binding consensus regarding basic deal terms, at which time access will broaden to include all other information concerning the business, properties and personnel (subject to restrictions imposed by 3 applicable law) of Captaris and its Subsidiaries as Vector may reasonably request and that is in the possession or control of Captaris.
perturbation.changed_text: Captaris agrees that, for a period ending eight weeks after the date of this letter agreement, it will endeavor to afford Vector and its Representatives, what Captaris reasonably considers as reasonable access to the information described in Exhibit A and the books, contracts, commitments and records of Captaris and its subsidiaries relevant thereto, unless and until Vector and Captaris have reached a non-binding consensus regarding basic deal terms, at which time access will broaden to include all other information concerning the business, properties and personnel (subject to restrictions imposed by 3 applicable law) of Captaris and its Subsidiaries as Vector may reasonably request and that is in the possession or control of Captaris.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will afford reasonable access' to 'will endeavor to afford what Captaris reasonably considers as reasonable access' introduces a subjective element, allowing Captaris to define what is 'reasonable'. This contradicts the earlier assurance that Vector will have 'reasonable access' for the purpose of evaluation. Now the determination of reasonable access will come from Captaris.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vector hereby agrees that it and its Representatives shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible transaction between the parties, that the Evaluation Material will be kept confidential and that Vector and its Representatives will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however, that (i) Vector may make any disclosure of such information to which Captaris gives its prior written consent and (ii) any of such information may be disclosed by Vector to its Representatives who need to know such information for the sole purpose of evaluating and implementing a possible transaction between the parties, who shall keep such information confidential and who are provided with a copy of this letter agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement from affording Vector 'reasonable access' to affording 'what Captaris reasonably considers as reasonable access.' This introduces a subjective element controlled by Captaris, directly conflicting with the implicit understanding in the 'Contradicted Text' that Vector's evaluation will be based on genuinely reasonable access. This subjective change now allows Captaris to limit access based on its own definition of 'reasonable', hindering Vector's ability to thoroughly evaluate a possible transaction, contradicting the purpose outlined in the 'Contradicted Text'.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished by Captaris hereunder, Vector hereby agrees that, for a period of twelve months from the date hereof, it will not, and will not encourage or assist its affiliates to, solicit for employment (a) any officers or other senior key employees of Captaris or any of its business units so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris, and (b) any of the other employees of Captaris or any of its business units with whom Vector has had contact or who Vector first became aware of during the period of its investigation of Captaris, so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished by Captaris hereunder, Vector hereby agrees that, for a period of twelve months from the date hereof, it will not, and will not encourage or assist its affiliates to, solicit for employment (a) any officers or other senior key employees of Captaris or any of its business units so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris, and (b) any of the other employees of Captaris or any of its business units with whom Vector has had contact or who Vector first became aware of during the period of its investigation of Captaris, so long as they are employed by the Captaris or its business units, without obtaining the prior written consent of Captaris, unless they were openly looking for a job in public platforms.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an exception to the non-solicitation agreement: 'unless they were openly looking for a job in public platforms.' This contradicts the original agreement, which strictly prohibits solicitation of listed employees without prior written consent. By allowing solicitation of employees actively seeking employment, it creates ambiguity about the enforceability of the non-solicitation clause. The exception is especially a contradiction to the later statement that the employees should not be hired, regardless if they are applying at any public platform.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing in the foregoing clause (b) shall preclude Vector from hiring any person who (i) initiates discussions with Vector regarding such employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by Vector, (ii) has ceased to be employed by Captaris prior to commencement of direct or indirect employment discussions between Vector and such person, or (iii) responds to any general solicitation placed by Vector or any recruitment agency on Vector’s behalf.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the non-solicitation agreement, allowing Vector to solicit employees who are openly looking for a job on public platforms. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that Vector is not precluded from hiring someone who initiates discussions without solicitation, has ceased employment with Captaris, or responds to a general solicitation. The changed text creates a contradiction because it allows solicitation of employees still employed by Captaris under specific circumstances (actively seeking a job), while the contradicted text only allows hiring of those who initiate contact, are no longer employed by Captaris, or respond to general solicitations, which are all different situations. The addition of 'unless they were openly looking for a job in public platforms' directly conflicts with the conditions outlined in the contradicted text, creating ambiguity and a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Vector agrees that, for a period (the “ Standstill Period ”) ending on the earliest of (i) six months from the date of this agreement and (ii) the occurrence of a “Significant Event” (as defined below), unless it shall have been specifically invited in writing by Captaris, neither Vector nor any of its affiliates (as such term is defined under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) will in any manner, directly or indirectly, (a) effect or publicly seek, offer or propose to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or publicly seek, offer or propose to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of 5% or more of the securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or consolidated assets (including securities of subsidiaries) of Captaris; (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving Captaris or any of its subsidiaries; (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Captaris or any of its subsidiaries; or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of Captaris; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the 1934 Act) the purpose of which is to effect a transaction described in (a) above; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to publicly seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of Captaris; or (d) take any action which would reasonably be expected to force Captaris to make a public announcement regarding any of’ the types of matters set forth in (a) above; provided, however, that, notwithstanding the foregoing, actions otherwise prohibited by clause (a)(iv) above shall be permitted hereunder on the date thirty days prior to the last day on which shareholders may properly nominate directors for election at, and properly bring (but, for the avoidance of doubt, not by means of Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act) other business to be transacted at, Captaris’s 2008 annual meeting of shareholders if such date occurs earlier than the expiration of the Standstill Period as a result of (x) the date of such meeting being advanced to be more than 30 days prior to the anniversary of Captaris’s 2007 annual meeting of shareholders and/or (y) an amendment to Captaris’s Amended and Restated Bylaws.
perturbation.changed_text: Vector agrees that, for a period (the “ Standstill Period ”) ending on the earliest of (i) six months from the date of this agreement and (ii) the occurrence of a “Significant Event” (as defined below), unless it shall have been specifically invited in writing by Captaris, neither Vector nor any of its affiliates (as such term is defined under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended (the “ Exchange Act ”)) will in any manner, directly or indirectly, (a) effect or publicly seek, offer or propose to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or publicly seek, offer or propose to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of 5% or more of the securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or consolidated assets (including securities of subsidiaries) of Captaris; (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving Captaris or any of its subsidiaries; (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to Captaris or any of its subsidiaries; or (iv) any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of Captaris; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the 1934 Act) the purpose of which is to effect a transaction described in (a) above; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to privately seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of Captaris; or (d) take any action which would reasonably be expected to force Captaris to make a public announcement regarding any of’ the types of matters set forth in (a) above; provided, however, that, notwithstanding the foregoing, actions otherwise prohibited by clause (a)(iv) above shall be permitted hereunder on the date thirty days prior to the last day on which shareholders may properly nominate directors for election at, and properly bring (but, for the avoidance of doubt, not by means of Rule 14a-8 under the Exchange Act) other business to be transacted at, Captaris’s 2008 annual meeting of shareholders if such date occurs earlier than the expiration of the Standstill Period as a result of (x) the date of such meeting being advanced to be more than 30 days prior to the anniversary of Captaris’s 2007 annual meeting of shareholders and/or (y) an amendment to Captaris’s Amended and Restated Bylaws.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'publicly seek to control or influence' to 'privately seek to control or influence' weakens the standstill agreement. The modified text allows for behind-the-scenes attempts to influence management, contradicting the overall intent of the standstill agreement to prevent Vector from taking actions to influence or control Captaris. This is contradictory to not having an agreement until final definitive agreement is executed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties understand and agree that no contract or agreement providing for any transaction between them shall be deemed to exist unless and until a final definitive agreement has been executed and delivered, and each party hereby waives, in advance, any claims (including, without limitation, breach of contract) in connection with any transaction involving the other party unless and until both parties shall have entered into a final definitive agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by weakening the standstill agreement, allowing 'private' attempts to influence management, whereas the original text stated 'publicly'. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which states that no agreement exists until a final agreement is made. Therefore the 'private' attempts to influence management contradicts the intention of not having an agreement until it is definitive and final. This is an instance of ambiguities leading to an in-text contradiction.
